task_completion.description: Create a Project Template named 'Standard Wind Installation' with exactly three tasks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Project Template screen showing the name 'Standard Wind Installation' and a list of tasks containing exactly 'Site Survey', 'Foundation Construction', and 'Turbine Erection'.

task_completion.description: Create and submit a Sales Order for customer 'Apex Energy' for 1 unit of 'Wind Farm Installation Service'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Sales Order screen showing customer 'Apex Energy', item 'Wind Farm Installation Service' (qty 1), and a status indicating it has been submitted (e.g., 'To Deliver and Bill' or a 'Cancel'/'Amend' button visible instead of 'Submit').

task_completion.description: Generate a linked Project from the Sales Order.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Project screen showing a reference/link to the Sales Order, or a Sales Order screen showing a linked Project in its dashboard/connections.

task_completion.description: The generated Project has the tasks auto-populated from the template.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Project screen or its Task list showing the tasks 'Site Survey', 'Foundation Construction', and 'Turbine Erection' associated with the new project.
The Task screen for 'Site Survey' (linked to the new project) showing its status as 'Completed'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the ERPNext web interface to complete the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the ERPNext web application in a browser, with forms being filled out and buttons being clicked.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows or python scripts are visible being used to create the records via API.

integrity.description: The agent actually entered the data into the ERPNext system.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual ERPNext forms (Project Template, Sales Order, Project, Task) with the required data, rather than a text editor or mockups.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Customer 'Apex Energy' and item 'Wind Farm Installation Service' are pre-existing in the system. The verifier checks for a template containing tasks 'site survey', 'foundation construction', and 'turbine erection' (case-insensitive). It expects a linked project generated from the Sales Order, with the 'site survey' task marked as Completed.
